speaker: seller

args.message: Good news on that front — Toyota's factory warranty on this 2018 model does have some coverage remaining, depending on mileage thresholds. At 71,000 miles, we're a bit past the bumper-to-bumper, but the powertrain warranty — which covers the hybrid system, engine, and transmission — that's still solid through 100,000 miles or 8 years from original purchase. That's one of the big advantages of buying a Toyota; they stand behind their vehicles. As for extended warranties, I can definitely set you up with an affordable aftermarket option if you want extra peace of mind, but honestly, with this car's maintenance record, I don't think you'll need it. What matters most to you in terms of coverage?

args.message: I'm interested in knowing the specifics of the powertrain warranty. What exactly does it cover, and what is not included in that coverage?

text: I'm interested in knowing the specifics of the powertrain warranty. What exactly does it cover, and what is not included in that coverage?

speaker: seller

args.message: Smart question. The powertrain warranty covers the engine, transmission, drivetrain components, and the hybrid battery system — basically all the critical stuff that keeps this car running. It's bumper-to-bumper protection on those major systems through 100,000 miles or 8 years. What's not covered would be wear-and-tear items like brake pads, wiper blades, filters, and routine maintenance — that's standard across all manufacturer warranties. Since this Prius has been so well-maintained, you shouldn't have any issues activating that coverage if anything ever does come up. But honestly, these hybrids are rock-solid. I've sold dozens of them, and powertrain claims are rare. Are you still interested in taking it for a test drive?
